Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Two studies in compiler optimisations 115 upvotes, 16 comments
  2. Personal Encyclopedias 589 upvotes, 121 comments
  3. Obsolete Sounds 141 upvotes, 25 comments
  4. French e, è, é, ê, ë – what's the difference? 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 37 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. European Parliament decided that Chat Control 1.0 must stop 437 upvotes, 105 comments
  8. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 69 upvotes, 11 comments
  9. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1351 upvotes, 364 comments
  10. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 185 upvotes, 48 comments
  11. Cory Doctorow: Interoperability Can Save the Open Web 45 upvotes, 8 comments
  12. End of "Chat Control": EU Parliament Stops Mass Surveillance in Voting Thriller 242 upvotes, 70 comments
  13. Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Social-Media Addiction Trial 36 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 755 upvotes, 254 comments
  15. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 202 upvotes, 60 comments
  16. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  17. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 189 upvotes, 66 comments
  18. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 329 upvotes, 149 comments
  19. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 188 upvotes, 94 comments
  20. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 193 upvotes, 106 comments
  21. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 150 upvotes, 84 comments
  22. Swift 6.3 225 upvotes, 134 comments
  23. ARC-AGI-3 460 upvotes, 296 comments
  24. Niche Museums 61 upvotes, 30 comments
  25. Landmark L.A. jury verdict finds Instagram, YouTube were designed to addict kids 227 upvotes, 169 comments
  26. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 215 upvotes, 177 comments
  27. What came after the 486? 110 upvotes, 88 comments
  28. SpaceStarCarz KoolWheelz Paper Models 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Newly purchased Vizio TVs now require Walmart accounts to use smart features 92 upvotes, 89 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments
